About: It is about the Jewish ruling dynasty of Judea during the Hellenistic times of the Second Temple period.
Affects: It affects the Jewish people in a crisis.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Second Temple period.
It is associated with classical antiquity.
It is associated with the Seleucid Empire.
It is associated with the Roman Republic.
It is associated with the Maccabean Revolt.
It is associated with the Herodian dynasty.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Hellenistic times.
It belongs to the Second Temple period.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Simon Thassi.
HasA: It has the Greek title basileus.
It has regional power status for several decades.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by ruling Judea semi-autonomously within the Seleucid Empire.
It is characterized by gaining further autonomy and expanding into neighboring regions.
It is characterized by the intervention of the Roman Republic.
It is characterized by becoming a client state.
It is characterized by a decline of power.
It is characterized by a civil war.
It is characterized by the installation of Herod the Great as king.
It is characterized by yielding to the Herodian dynasty.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Seleucid Empire.
It was influenced by the Roman Republic.
It was influenced by the Parthian Empire.
IsA: It is a Jewish ruling dynasty.
KnownAs: It is known as the Maccabees.
It is known as the Asmonean dynasty.
KnownFor: It is known for ruling Judea.
It is known for its rulers taking the Greek title basileus.
It is known for attaining regional power status.
OccurredBetween: It occurred between c. 141 and c. 116 BC.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Hellenistic times.
It occurred during the Second Temple period.
It occurred during the Maccabean Revolt.
It occurred during Roman civil wars.
It occurred during the Jewish–Roman wars of the 1st century.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Judea.
It occurred in Perea.
It occurred in Samaria.
It occurred in Idumea.
It occurred in Galilee.
It occurred in Iturea.
It occurred in Coele Syria.
It occurred in Phoenicia.
It occurred in Ptolemaic Egypt.
It occurred in Antioch.
OccurredAround: It occurred around 141 BC.
It occurred around 110 BC.
It occurred around 168–167 BC.
It occurred around 175 BC.
It occurred around 167 BC.
It occurred around 160 BC.
It occurred around 175–164 BC.
It occurred around 170–168 BC.
It occurred around 63 BC.
It occurred around 40 BC.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the village of Heshmon.
It originated in the town of Mod'in.
PartOf: It is part of the Second Temple period.
It is part of classical antiquity.
It is part of the Seleucid Empire.
It is part of the Roman Republic.
It is part of the Roman province of Judaea.
UsedAs: It is used as a client state.
It is used as a Greek polis.
It is used as a Roman province.
UsedFor: It is used for ruling Judea.
It is used for expanding into neighboring regions.
It is used for maintaining regional power status.
It is used for political intrigue.
It is used for bribing the king.
It is used for suppressing Jewish and Samaritan religious and cultural observances.
It is used for imposing Hellenistic practices.
It is used for regaining autonomy.
It is used for a proxy war.
It is used for bolstering legitimacy.
UsedIn: It is used in Judea.
It is used in Perea.
It is used in Samaria.
It is used in Idumea.
It is used in Galilee.
It is used in Iturea.
It is used in Coele Syria.
It is used in Phoenicia.
It is used in Ptolemaic Egypt.
It is used in Antioch.
UsedTo: It is used to assert strict control.
It is used to suppress Jewish and Samaritan religious and cultural observances.
It is used to impose Hellenistic practices.
It is used to regain some autonomy.
It is used to relax Rome's grip.
It is used to allow a brief reassertion of autonomy.
It is used to crush autonomy.
It is used to bolster the legitimacy of his reign.
It is used to drown the last male Hasmonean heir.
